Add before it:
```csharp
[RequiresUnreferencedCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values is not supported with trimming. Use the Configuration Binder Source Generator (EnableConfigurationBindingGenerator=true) instead.")]
[RequiresDynamicCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values requires generating dynamic code at runtime, for example instantiating generic types.")]
```

Method 2 (line 27):
```csharp
public static IAzureClientBuilder<ArmClient, ArmClientOptions> AddArmClient<TBuilder, TConfiguration>(this TBuilder builder, TConfiguration configuration)
```
Add before it:
```csharp
[RequiresUnreferencedCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values is not supported with trimming. Use the Configuration Binder Source Generator (EnableConfigurationBindingGenerator=true) instead.")]
[RequiresDynamicCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values requires generating dynamic code at runtime, for example instantiating generic types.")]
```

### 4. ArmEnvironment.cs
Replace the `ToString()` method (line 80):
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this);
```
with:
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this, ArmJsonSerializerContext.Default.ArmEnvironment);
```
Add `using Azure.ResourceManager.Serialization;` to the usings if not present (add it near the other usings at the top of the file).

### 5. ArmClientOptions.cs
Replace the JsonSerializer.Deserialize call at line 52:
```csharp
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
```
with:
```csharp
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize(span, ArmJsonSerializerContext.Default.DictionaryStringDictionaryStringJsonElement);
```
Add `using Azure.ResourceManager.Serialization;` to the usings if not present.

### 6. Create the JsonSerializerContext file
Create a new file at `/tmp/sv-ed40a1332c394118be195fb07a7dc38c/before/Serialization/ArmJsonSerializerContext.cs` with this content:

```csharp
// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
// Licensed under the MIT License.

using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Text.Json;
using System.Text.Json.Serialization;

namespace Azure.ResourceManager.Serialization
{
    [JsonSerializerContext]
    [JsonSerializable(typeof(ArmEnvironment))]
    [JsonSerializable(typeof(Dictionary<string, string>))]
    [JsonSerializable(typeof(Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>))]
    internal partial class ArmJsonSerializerContext : JsonSerializerContext
    {
    }
}
```

Make sure to create the `Serialization` directory first if it doesn't exist.

### 7. Fix 4 ArmOperation files with ToObjectFromJson
Replace the `ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>()` calls in these 4 files. They all have the same pattern at line 61:

```csharp
var lroDetails = ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>();
```

Replace with:
```csharp
var lroDetails = JsonSerializer.Deserialize(ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToString(), ArmJsonSerializerContext.Default.DictionaryStringString);
```

- `/tmp/sv-ed40a1332c394118be195fb07a7dc38c/before/ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperationOfT.cs`

- `/tmp/sv-ed40a1332c394118be195fb07a7dc38c/before/Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperationOfT.cs`

kind.prompt:
Fix AOT compatibility warnings in serialization files in the project at /tmp/sv-ed40a1332c394118be195fb07a7dc38c/before. Make these specific edits using the `edit` tool.

## Context
All types being serialized/deserialized have `IJsonModel<T>` implementations with static `DeserializeX(JsonElement)` methods and `WriteObjectValue` extension support. Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize` calls with AOT-safe equivalents.

## Pattern A: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, X)` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(X, options)`
In each case, the `writer` is a `Utf8JsonWriter` and `options` is the `ModelReaderWriterOptions` variable available in the method scope.

## Pattern B: Replace `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>(property.Value.GetRawText())` with the static `DeserializeT(property.Value)` method

## Files and changes:

### 1. Resources/Generated/GenericResourceData.Serialization.cs
Read the file first, then make these edits:

Line 45: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Plan)` → `writer.WriteObjectValue(Plan, options)`
Line 77: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Identity)` → `writer.WriteObjectValue(Identity, options)`
Line 142: `plan = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ArmPlan>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `plan = ArmPlan.DeserializeArmPlan(property.Value)`
Line 179: `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value)`
Line 211: `extendedLocation = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ExtendedLocation>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `extendedLocation = ExtendedLocation.DeserializeExtendedLocation(property.Value)`
Line 254: `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value)`

### 2. Resources/Generated/PolicyAssignmentData.Serialization.cs
Line 50: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ManagedIdentity)` → `writer.WriteObjectValue(ManagedIdentity, options)`
Line 201: `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value)`
Line 225: `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value)`

### 3. Resources/Generated/Models/PolicyAssignmentPatch.Serialization.cs
Line 46: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Identity)` → `writer.WriteObjectValue(Identity, options)`
Line 131: `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value)`

### 4. Common/Generated/Models/OperationStatusResult.Serialization.cs
Line 84: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Error)` → `writer.WriteObjectValue(Error, options)`
Line 173: `array.Add(JsonSerializer.Deserialize<OperationStatusResult>(item.GetRawText()))` → `array.Add(DeserializeOperationStatusResult(item))`
  Note: `item` is a `JsonElement` from `property.Value.EnumerateArray()`, and `DeserializeOperationStatusResult` is a static method in the same class.
Line 184: `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ResponseError>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize(property.Value.GetRawText(), Azure.Core.Serialization.AzureCoreContext.Default.ResponseError)`
  WAIT - actually, ResponseError is from Azure.Core. For ResponseError, use `writer.WriteObjectValue` for serialize, but for deserialize we need an AOT-safe approach. Let me reconsider this one.
  
  Actually, for ResponseError deserialization, the best approach is to use `ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>`:
  `error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json)`
  
  BUT this may also have trimming warnings. Let me take a different approach:
  
  Since ResponseError implements `IJsonModel<ResponseError>`, use the interface. Create a Utf8JsonReader from the JSON:
  ```csharp
  var responseErrorBinaryData = BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText());
  error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(responseErrorBinaryData);
  ```
  
  Actually, simplest: just leave it as is for now, and let me handle ResponseError differently. Replace it with:
  `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize(property.Value.GetRawText(), Azure.ResourceManager.Serialization.ArmJsonSerializerContext.Default.ResponseError)`
  BUT ResponseError isn't in our context...
  
  OK, let me use a simpler approach. For ResponseError deserialization, use the IJsonModel interface:
  ```csharp
  error = ((IJsonModel<ResponseError>)new ResponseError(null, null)).Create(ref new Utf8JsonReader(property.Value.GetRawText()...));
  ```
  This is complex. Let me just skip ResponseError deserialization for now. The user managing this task will handle it separately.

UPDATED INSTRUCTIONS FOR ResponseError deserialization (lines 184 in OperationStatusResult.Serialization.cs and line 113 in ResourceGroupExportResult.Serialization.cs):
DO NOT change ResponseError deserialization lines. Leave them as-is for now. I will handle them separately.

### 5. Resources/Generated/Models/ResourceGroupExportResult.Serialization.cs
Line 53: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Error)` → `writer.WriteObjectValue(Error, options)`
Line 113: DO NOT CHANGE (ResponseError deserialization - will be handled separately)

### 6. Resources/Generated/Models/TrackedResourceExtendedData.Serialization.cs
Line 44: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ExtendedLocation)` → `writer.WriteObjectValue(ExtendedLocation, options)`
Line 85: `extendedLocation = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ExtendedLocation>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `extendedLocation = ExtendedLocation.DeserializeExtendedLocation(property.Value)`
Line 128: `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText())` → `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value)`

### 7. SystemData-only deserialize files (same pattern for all):
Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText())` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value)`

Files:
- Resources/Generated/ManagementLockData.Serialization.cs (line 116)
- Resources/Generated/PolicyDefinitionData.Serialization.cs (line 158)
- Resources/Generated/PolicySetDefinitionData.Serialization.cs (line 161)
- Resources/Generated/ResourceGroupData.Serialization.cs (line 140)
- Resources/Generated/FeatureData.Serialization.cs (line 107)
- Resources/Generated/TagResourceData.Serialization.cs (line 100)
- Resources/Generated/DataPolicyManifestData.Serialization.cs (line 177)
- ManagementGroup/Generated/ManagementGroupData.Serialization.cs (line 132)
- ManagementGroup/Generated/ManagementGroupSubscriptionData.Serialization.cs (line 126)
- ManagementGroup/Generated/Models/DescendantData.Serialization.cs (line 120)
- ManagementGroup/Generated/Models/EntityData.Serialization.cs (line 229)

### 8. ManagementGroup/Generated/Models/EntityData.Serialization.cs (additional edits)
Line 71: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Parent)` → `writer.WriteObjectValue(Parent, options)` (Parent is SubResource which implements IJsonModel)
Line 267: `parent = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SubResource>(property0.Value.GetRawText())` → `parent = SubResource.DeserializeSubResource(property0.Value)`

### 9. Common/Custom/Models/ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs (SPECIAL HANDLING)

Line 31: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ManagedServiceIdentityType, jOptions)` with inline converter logic:
```csharp
writer.WritePropertyName("type"u8);
writer.WriteStringValue(ManagedServiceIdentityType.ToString());
```

Line 49: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, item.Value, jOptions)` with:
```csharp
writer.WriteObjectValue(item.Value, options);
```

Line 206: Replace `type = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentityType>($"{{{property}}}");` with:
```csharp
type = new ManagedServiceIdentityType(property.Value.GetString());
```

Line 218: Replace `dictionary.Add(new ResourceIdentifier(property0.Name), JsonSerializer.Deserialize<UserAssignedIdentity>(property0.Value.GetRawText()));` with:
```csharp
dictionary.Add(new ResourceIdentifier(property0.Name), UserAssignedIdentity.DeserializeUserAssignedIdentity(property0.Value));
```

IMPORTANT: Read each file before editing to verify the exact content at each line. Use the `edit` tool for all changes. Match the exact text including whitespace.
